payload.text: Make this real using vite with Vue and meta-framewrok next.js/nuxt

**Considering React Architecture**

I'm currently focused on the React SPA architecture. Key considerations involve a component-based design, state management strategies (likely Redux or Context API), and routing options for optimal user experience. I'm also thinking about API integration and data fetching methods.


**Clarifying conflicting goals**

I'm now sorting through conflicting directives. My instructions prioritize React, TypeScript, Tailwind, and Gemini API. However, the user is requesting Vue with Next.js/Nuxt. It also describes a "TechStore" e-commerce app. I'm prioritizing the system goals, while noting the discrepancy. The user's store features are in mind: Product Discovery, Shopping Cart, and Paystack integration.


**Defining the Project Scope**

I've decided on a React 19 + Vite SPA, aligning with system instructions, and will build the "TechStore". Core modules are defined; a file structure will include components for header, hero, product display, cart, checkout, footer, and layout, with a cart store using a context hook. A mock API service and core types will support the product data as requested. I'll implement responsive design with Tailwind, and Paystack client-side integration via the public key.


**Refining Design Details**

I'm now diving into the design and tech details. I'll utilize `gemini-3-flash-preview` for search-related features and the "Ask Gemini about this product" idea. Color palette will be a blue primary with slate/gray text. Lucide-React will handle icons. The layout will be a centered 1440px container. I've defined product categories and associated fields. I'm choosing a HashRouter for `App.tsx` and focusing `CartContext` logic around item management and VAT calculation. Finally, there will be a validated Checkout form with a Paystack modal trigger.

payload.text: Add sorting options (e.g., price low-to-high, price high-to-low, rating) to the product listing.Create a product detail page that displays more information about a selected product and allows adding to cart.Enhance the search input to show real-time suggestions as the user types, using the Gemini API for smart search.Add an 'Add to Wishlist' button to the product cards and implement wishlist persistence using localStorage. Add functionality to filter products based on the search input in the header.

**Considering Application Enhancements**

I'm now focusing on specific enhancements for the `TechStore` application, specifically adding sorting functionality to the product listing. I am working on sorting by price (low to high and high to low) and rating. Next, I'll prioritize designing a detailed product information page.


**Developing Feature Roadmap**

My current focus is on building out the search features and incorporating a user wishlist. I've broken down the implementation into manageable tasks: introducing real-time search suggestions with the Gemini API, creating the wishlist functionality using localStorage, and implementing search filtering on user input. I am updating my data types and application state management to reflect these changes. I've designed several new components to add functionality to the product cards and overall UI.


**Implementing New Features**

I am now actively implementing the search, filtering, and wishlist functionality. My focus is on integrating the Gemini API for real-time search suggestions and developing the sorting mechanism. I have also started updating `types.ts` to include the `WISHLIST` view. I'm prioritizing user experience by creating a sidebar for the wishlist. The UI design includes a new search suggestion dropdown in the layout and a heart icon next to the cart for wishlist access. I am using `gemini-3-flash-preview` for real-time search and client-side sorting. I am using `useEffect` for cart and wishlist persistence.


**Refining Code Implementation**

I'm now integrating the search suggestions using `geminiService.ts` and building the `ProductDetail` and `Wishlist` components. Updating `App.tsx` handles main logic. The `Layout.tsx` incorporates search and wishlist triggers. Finally, `ProductCard.tsx` now features a wishlist button and click functionality for product details. I'm focusing on React, since that's what's currently provided. I am implementing the changes with this assumption.

entries.description: Added PRODUCT_DETAIL and WISHLIST views, and updated Category type.
Added search suggestions function using Gemini API.
Enhanced layout with search suggestions and wishlist trigger.
Updated ProductCard with wishlist toggle and detail navigation.
New component for displaying detailed product information.
New component for the wishlist sidebar.
Implemented filtering, sorting, product detail navigation, and wishlist functionality.

payload.text:
**Pinpointing the Error**

I'm now zeroing in on the specific error in `App.tsx`. The problem is occurring on line 221, within the `else` block of the `filteredAndSortedProducts.length > 0` condition. It seems that `Search` is undefined there. I'm focusing on the context within that `else` block to understand why.


**Identifying the Missing Import**

I've pinpointed the root cause! The `Search` component, used within the conditional rendering on line 221 of `App.tsx`, was not imported from `lucide-react`. I had overlooked that, and now have fixed the problem.


**Addressing the Library Errors**

I'm now integrating the `Search` component into `App.tsx`'s import statements. Furthermore, I'm now shifting focus to `services/geminiService.ts`, making sure the `@google/genai` library initialization adheres to the specified guidelines. The `GoogleGenAI` instance must use only `process.env.API_KEY`, which must be supplied directly to the `apiKey` property. This will be integrated and verified.


**Refining the API Interaction**

I'm now focusing on `services/geminiService.ts` and the precise usage of the Google GenAI library. I need to ensure the initialization of the `GoogleGenAI` instance is strictly adhering to the instruction, which is to use only `process.env.API_KEY` directly, without any fallback. I've been examining the code for deviations. I should also address the error in `App.tsx` first.


**Prioritizing the Critical Error**

I am now returning to the original error. I was attempting to verify compliance with Gemini guidelines, but the `Search` component is still undefined. I should add `Search` to the import statement. It appears to be an easy fix. I will take care of this and get back to the Gemini code.



**Verifying the Dependencies**

I'm now double-checking the dependencies, starting with an audit of `App.tsx`. I noticed `ChevronRight` is imported but unused, which is okay. Now, I have been checking the `services/geminiService.ts` file, and I am ensuring the Google GenAI library is initialized as per the latest guidelines, specifically the use of `ai.models.generateContent` directly. I also confirmed that the API key is used correctly. Finally, I confirm that the model "gemini-3-flash-preview" is correct for text tasks. I will now revisit the `App.tsx` file for the import statements.


**Refining the Imports**

I'm now revisiting `App.tsx` and ensuring all necessary components are imported. I'm confirming that `Search`, used on line 221, is indeed imported. I also reviewed other imports and usages to ensure completeness, checking `ChevronRight` (unused), and confirming various icons were correct, like `CheckCircle2`, `ShieldCheck`, `Truck`, `CreditCard`, `ArrowLeft`, and `Loader2`. I'll address these imports in the XML.


**Confirming the Imports**

I'm now zeroing in on the import statements within `App.tsx`, and ensuring all components used are correctly imported from `lucide-react`. I've confirmed that `Search` is, indeed, required on line 221 and needs inclusion in the import list. I'm satisfied that this resolves the previously reported error. I will now examine `services/geminiService.ts` to see what further updates are needed there.


**Reviewing the Component Imports**

I'm now back in `App.tsx` and meticulously reviewing the component imports. I'm verifying that all components used within the application are correctly imported from `lucide-react`, which includes items like `Search`, `SlidersHorizontal`, and all the other icons. My main goal is to be certain all necessary components, including `Search`, are correctly declared in the import statements to prevent any further errors during rendering.


**Examining the State Variables**

I am now focusing on the state management within `App.tsx`, and I'm ensuring the local storage persistence logic for cart and wishlist data is correctly implemented. I am verifying the `useEffect` hooks for cart and wishlist synchronization, confirming data retrieval and storage are working as intended. I am making sure the state updates are correctly tied to the local storage events.


**Analyzing the Filtering**

I'm now examining the filtering logic within the `App.tsx` file, specifically the `useMemo` hook that manages product display. I'm reviewing the filtering by category, search query, and sorting criteria to ensure all aspects are functioning correctly and efficiently. I'm paying close attention to the interaction between `activeCategory`, `searchQuery`, and `sortBy` to avoid any logical inconsistencies.


**Evaluating State Transitions**

I'm now deep-diving into the state management within `App.tsx`, and I'm scrutinizing the `addToCart`, `handleToggleWishlist`, `handleUpdateQuantity`, and `handleRemoveItem` functions. I'm confirming these functions correctly update the cart and wishlist states, and also verify the local storage updates. My main focus is confirming proper and accurate data modification and management.


**Verifying the Checkout Logic**

I am now focusing on the checkout process within `App.tsx`, and verifying the `handleCheckout` function, which currently has a `setTimeout` function. I am verifying that the form submission is correctly handled and that the state updates related to processing, reference number generation, view transitions, and cart clearing are functioning correctly. I will also check the delay introduced by the `setTimeout` function.


**Validating the Display**

I'm now zeroing in on the UI display within the `<Products />` component. I'm focusing on how search queries and filtering impact the display, specifically ensuring correct product counts and labels. I'm verifying the conditional rendering within the JSX, especially the `h2` and `p` tags related to search results and item counts.


**Analyzing the Sorting**

I'm now examining the sorting logic in the `<Products />` component, particularly how the `sortBy` state variable influences the product order. I'm reviewing the `select` element and `onChange` handler, ensuring the correct `SortOption` values are being captured and that the product list is properly re-rendered after sorting. The options within the `select` element are also checked.



**Refining the Error Display**

I am now focusing on the product display within `App.tsx` and verifying the conditional rendering logic related to the filtered products' count. I am confirming the correct message is displayed when the filtered list is empty, and I am also ensuring that the display updates accurately when there are products to show. My current goal is to ensure the user feedback is consistent with the data displayed.



**Reviewing the Product Display**

I'm now focusing on the no-products display within the UI, specifically ensuring the logic handles empty results accurately. I am double-checking the conditional rendering and verifying that the correct messages are displayed to guide the user in adjusting their filters or search. I'm reviewing the "Clear all filters" button functionality.



**Verifying the Navigation**

I'm now integrating a "Return to Store" button at the start of the checkout section. I've focused on the appearance and functionality of this button within `App.tsx`, and I'm ensuring it correctly redirects the user back to the home view upon click. The ArrowLeft icon integration is confirmed as well.


**Validating the Form**

I am now meticulously verifying the structure and functionality of the checkout form within the `App.tsx` file. I am reviewing each input field for required attributes and correct placeholder texts, making sure that it adheres to the original design and expectations. I'm focusing on validating the email, first name, and last name fields.



**Validating the Shipping Form**

I am now carefully checking the shipping address form fields, ensuring they have the correct labels, are required, and have appropriate placeholders like "123 Technology Way" and "Lagos". I'm making sure all elements are set up according to the original design requirements. I'm focusing on the input type and any validation applied to the inputs.


**Validating the Payment Form**

I am now carefully checking the payment section within the checkout process, specifically the credit card form. I am making sure the layout and inputs meet expectations, and that the card number field, expiry date, and CVV inputs adhere to the required formatting.



**Examining the Order Summary**

I'm now diving into the "Order Summary" section within the checkout process. I'm focusing on the display of cart items, ensuring they render correctly, including images and details. I'm double-checking the layout, responsiveness, and potential scrolling behavior for long lists. I'm also ensuring the scrollbar is hidden using the `scrollbar-hide` class.


**Analyzing the Display**

I'm now examining the layout of the order summary and the correctness of the total calculations. Specifically, I'm checking to make sure that the cart items are correctly displayed, including their names, quantities, and prices. I'm focusing on validating the display of subtotal, VAT, shipping, and total amounts at the bottom of the summary, and how these totals are correctly calculated.


**Validating the Totals**

I'm now focusing on the `calculateTotals()` function within `App.tsx` and ensuring the total calculation, including subtotal, VAT, and shipping, is accurate. I'm verifying that the displayed total matches the calculated sum of cart items, VAT, and shipping. I'm also confirming the `toFixed(2)` method renders the amounts correctly.



**Confirming the Confirmation Display**

I am now carefully checking the success confirmation view after checkout within `App.tsx`. I am verifying the display of the checkmark icon, the confirmation message, and the reference number, ensuring proper rendering after a successful payment. Also, I'm ensuring that clicking the "Continue Shopping" button transitions the application back to the home view as intended.



**Verifying the Sidebars**

I am now carefully checking the cart and wishlist sidebars within `App.tsx`. I am confirming the correct state management for their open/close states using `isCartOpen` and `isWishlistOpen`, and ensuring the sidebars properly render and function when their respective states are true. I am verifying data binding and event handlers within both components, and also checking their visibility.



**Refining the API Logic**

I am now focusing on refining the API interaction logic within `services/geminiService.ts`. I'm meticulously checking the `getSmartSearch` function, including ensuring that the Gemini API is correctly called. I am focusing on making the call to the API consistent and correct. I am confirming proper handling of the response and potential errors. I am also making sure that the data transformations are also correct.

entries.description: Add Search to lucide-react imports to fix the error on line 221.
Update GoogleGenAI initialization and content generation to follow strict @google/genai guidelines.

payload.text: Enhance accessibility by adding ARIA labels and ensuring keyboard navigation is fully supported. Improve the checkout UI by adding progress indicators and clear call-to-actions for each step. Allow users to leave reviews and ratings for purchased products. Add an order tracking feature where users can enter their order ID to see the status of their shipment. Integrate additional payment gatewaysto paystack to the checkout flow.

**Considering New Features**

I'm currently focused on enhancing the e-commerce store. Accessibility is a top priority, planning to implement ARIA labels and robust keyboard navigation. Also, I'm thinking about a streamlined checkout UI, including progress indicators and clear calls to action at each step. Reviews and ratings will allow user feedback.


**Implementing Order Tracking**

I've been working on order tracking. I'm adding a feature where users can input their order ID and track their shipment status via a timeline. Also, accessibility remains a key focus; I'm adding `aria-*` attributes and managing focus within modals and sidebars. I'm implementing a step-by-step checkout process with a visual stepper, too.


**Refining Data Structures**

I'm now refining the data structures. I've defined `Review` with `id`, `productId`, `userName`, `rating`, `comment`, and `date`. Also, I've created an `OrderStatus` enum and an `Order` interface, including `id`, `items`, `total`, `status`, and `date` properties. I'm expanding my approach to include a new payment option, probably Stripe or PayPal, within the checkout process.

entries.description: Add review, order, and view types for enhanced features.
Enhance accessibility with ARIA labels and add Order Tracking link.
Add interactive reviews section with submission form.
Add ARIA roles and improve keyboard accessibility for the Cart sidebar.
Add Checkout progress steps, payment gateway options, and Order Tracking logic.

payload.text: Enhance the empty cart state in the Cart component to include a clear call to action button to encourage shopping.

**Revising Cart UX**

I'm currently focused on the empty cart state in `Cart.tsx`. I noticed that the component already has a "Start Shopping" button, so I'm evaluating how to best integrate it into the UX. My aim is to ensure the call-to-action is clear and compelling.


**Refining Button Clarity**

I'm now revisiting the "Start Shopping" button within the `Cart.tsx` component. The current implementation seems sufficient at first glance, but I am now evaluating how to make the call-to-action more prominent and persuasive. I plan to enhance the visuals with additional text, and potentially improve the button's styling or add an icon to its design.


**Improving Button's Placement**

I'm now focusing on the "Start Shopping" button's visual weight and placement within `Cart.tsx`. Considering adjustments to ensure it's the primary call-to-action in the empty cart state. I'm aiming for a more compelling user experience and less ambiguity, so I'm thinking of adding subtle animations to draw attention to it.


**Calculating Cart Totals**

I've just added the subtotal, VAT calculation, and total calculations to this cart. I've also incorporated a `useEffect` hook to manage `document.body.style.overflow` based on the cart's open/closed state, ensuring that scrolling is disabled when the cart is open. Now I'm rendering a fixed-position cart, using the `isOpen` state to conditionally render the cart's UI.


**Evaluating Cart Header**

I'm now revisiting the cart header. I'm focusing on the "Your Cart" title and the item count display within the `Cart.tsx` component. I want to ensure the visual hierarchy is clear and that the item count is easily noticeable. I'm also considering accessibility improvements for screen readers.


**Refining Empty State Message**

I'm now refining the empty state message within `Cart.tsx`. I think the current phrasing could be more inviting, so I'm evaluating alternative wording to encourage users to start shopping. I'm also planning to explore the use of a more dynamic approach, perhaps displaying product category recommendations instead of a generic message.


**Reviewing Cart Items**

I'm now diving into the cart items display within the component. I'm focusing on the rendering of individual items, particularly their visual presentation and interactions. The current layout seems functional, however, I'm thinking about enhancing the visual hierarchy, maybe adding a subtle animation to the item's name on hover. I'm also going to be evaluating the `aria-label` attribute on the remove button, to make sure it's helpful.


**Assessing Quantity Controls**

I am now closely examining the quantity controls for each item within `Cart.tsx`. I'm going through the plus and minus buttons, focusing on their functionality, accessibility, and visual presentation, specifically the `aria-label` on the buttons. I am also planning to improve the user experience of the controls, and potentially add visual cues.


**Enhancing Quantity Display**

I'm now refining the quantity display within each cart item, specifically evaluating the styling and presentation of the quantity controls. I've focused on the appearance of the "+" and "-" buttons, aiming for a more intuitive and visually appealing design. I also plan to improve responsiveness for easier interaction.

entries.description: Enhance empty cart state with better visuals and a more compelling call-to-action button.
